title: 2 women attempt to swim from Marblehead to Scituate to qualify for Ireland marathon swims
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Abigail Fairman and Sarah Roberts attempted an 18-mile swim from Marblehead to Scituate to qualify for Irish marathon swims.
They faced challenging wind and water conditions, ending the swim early but achieving the six-hour qualification requirement.
Fairman and Roberts plan to participate in the North Channel and Fastnet Swims in Ireland later this summer.

title: Preservation efforts ramp up as Swansea Friends Meeting House is named among nation’s most endangered historic sites
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Swansea Friends Meeting House in Somerset named one of 11 most endangered historic sites by National Trust for Historic Preservation.
A $184,300 grant from Mass Cultural Council boosts restoration efforts for the exterior of the centuries-old Quaker meeting house.
Upon completion of restoration, organizers aim to repurpose the meeting house as a community cultural center honoring its historical significance.

title: Cape Cod’s Sundae School to offer 76-cent ice cream scoops
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Sundae School will offer 76-cent ice cream scoops on June 14 to celebrate its 50th anniversary.
The promotion will run from 2 to 4 p.m. at both Harwich Port and Dennisport locations.
The 76-cent price reflects the year 1976, when Sundae School first opened its doors in Cape Cod.

title: July 4th fireworks cancellation sparks intense pushback in Rutland
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Rutland officials canceled July 4th fireworks, parade, and concert due to staffing limitations impacting public safety.
A failed tax override vote led to the inability to fund additional public safety personnel for Rutland's celebrations.
Residents criticize non-refundable donations, and a nonprofit calls for an investigation into Rutland's use of funds.

title: 2 teen girls arrested after Boston police allegedly find multiple firearms, drugs, and a knife in vehicle
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two teenage girls were arrested in Dorchester after Boston police found firearms, a knife, and drugs in their vehicle.
A 16-year-old from Taunton and Jacqueline Rose Botelho, 18, face multiple firearm and drug charges after the vehicle stop.
Botelho pled not guilty and is held at Suffolk County Jail; her next court date is June 22.

title: T pushing new PSA after fatal escalator incident
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: MBTA plans new PSA on escalator emergency stop buttons after a fatal accident at Davis Station in February.
Steven McCluskey's clothing became trapped in a Davis Station escalator, leading to his death after more than a week.
MBTA General Manager emphasized ongoing safety improvements and potential upgrades in surveillance and safety monitoring.

title: A 3BR cabin-style home near the Berkshires — can you guess the price?
author: Emily Turner
bullets: 3-bedroom cabin-style home near Berkshires sits on 14.6 acres, built in 1977, with 2,185 square feet.
Property includes 3 full baths and 1 half bath, with an annual tax of $6,396 in 2025.
Price options for the home are $825K, $625K, and $425K.

title: Mass. photographer stabbed in ‘unprovoked attack’ while shooting wedding in Maine
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Donald Halsing, 26, a Mendon, Mass. wedding photographer, was stabbed at Kingsley Camp Ground in Raymond, Maine.
Halsing suffered serious injuries, including a liver laceration, and underwent emergency surgery at Maine Medical Center.
Andrew Manderson, 26, a wedding guest, was arrested and charged with elevated aggravated assault in the unprovoked attack.

title: Conan O’Brien inspires grads, rips Trump administration at 2026 Harvard commencement
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Conan O'Brien delivered Harvard's 2026 commencement speech, blending humor with critiques of the Trump administration.
O'Brien joked he would sue Harvard over trivial issues, mocking similar actions by Trump's Justice Department.
He encouraged graduates to embrace community and humility despite a climate of narcissism and isolation.

title: Healey issues new ICE guidance for schools, hospitals, and places of worship
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey issued new guidance for schools, hospitals, and places of worship on handling ICE interactions.
The guidance includes designating contact points, protecting information, and requiring ICE agents to show warrants for nonpublic areas.
Healey's executive order bans civil arrests by ICE in nonpublic state areas and prevents new 287(g) immigration law agreements.
